authors: Marlis Stubenvoll Isabelle Freiling Jörg Matthes a Department of Media and Communications
raw_title: Resistant and Misinformed: Understanding the Role of Elite Perceptions and Resistance Strategies Against COVID-19 Reporting in Legacy Media in Adopting COVID-19 Misperceptions
title_en: Resistant and Misinformed: Understanding the Role of Elite Perceptions and Resistance Strategies Against COVID-19 Reporting in Legacy Media in Adopting COVID-19 Misperceptions
title_zh: 抗拒与误信：理解精英对传统媒体COVID-19报道的认知和抵抗策略在采纳COVID-19错误观念中的作用
plain_summary_zh: 这项研究探讨了为什么有些人会相信关于COVID-19的错误信息。它特别关注那些对传统媒体（如报纸、电视新闻）的COVID-19报道持怀疑或抗拒态度的人，尤其是精英群体。研究试图理解这些人的看法和抵抗策略如何导致他们更容易接受关于疫情的虚假或误导性信息。
plain_summary_en: This study explores why some people believe misinformation about COVID-19. It focuses specifically on individuals, particularly elites, who are skeptical or resistant to COVID-19 reporting in legacy media (like newspapers and TV news). The research aims to understand how these perceptions and resistance strategies make people more likely to adopt false or misleading information about the pandemic.
research_direction_zh: 媒体信任与健康错误信息传播：研究精英群体对传统媒体的态度如何影响公众对健康危机的认知和行为，特别是在疫情等公共卫生事件中。
research_direction_en: Media Trust and Health Misinformation Spread: Investigating how elite attitudes toward legacy media influence public perceptions and behaviors during health crises, particularly in events like pandemics.
